summary: Canonical end-to-end flows through the five-layer OpenADS engine (ABI / Session / SQL / Engine / Platform), plus its wire protocol and Studio HTTP console.

layers.name: ABI
layers.owns: extern "C" Ads* exports (ace32/64.dll, libace.so/.dylib); OEM/ANSI/UTF-8/UTF-16 conversion; ACE error code translation.

layers.name: Session
layers.owns: Connection, Statement, HandleRegistry, Tx stack, AEP procedure registry, encryption key.

layers.name: SQL
layers.owns: Lexer, Parser, Resolver, Planner, Executor; AEP host; xBase UDFs.

layers.name: Engine
layers.owns: Table, Index, MemoStore, Cursor, LockMgr, TxLog/WAL, Catalog; Driver trait.

layers.name: Platform
layers.owns: File, mmap, byte-range locks, sockets, DLL loader (Win32 + POSIX).

drivers.notes: Proprietary ADS format — out of scope.

flows.title: Local DBF access (in-process)
flows.summary: Harbour / Clipper / X# / C app links contrib/rddads (or calls AdsConnect60 directly) against ace64.dll; OpenADS opens DBF + CDX + FPT siblings on disk and serves rows entirely in the host process.

steps.action: auto-bind sibling .cdx (rc13) — every tag navigable without explicit AdsOpenIndex60
Cursor walks current order; LockMgr serialises shared/exclusive byte-range locks

flows.notes: Same DLL serves both this in-process mode and tcp:// remote mode — chosen by the URI shape passed to AdsConnect60.

flows.title: Remote TCP access (openads_serverd)
flows.summary: Same ace64.dll, given a tcp://host:port/<dir> URI, opens a TCP session to openads_serverd. The daemon runs L2–L5 in-process on the server box; the client DLL forwards every Ads* call as a wire-protocol opcode pair.

steps.action: Decode opcode, call ABI session, encode Ack frame
Ack frames (FetchAck batches matrix of rows — M12.11)

flows.notes: Strict ping-pong frame format: BE u32 length, u8 opcode, payload. One TCP connection = one session = one engine::Connection. TLS plug-in transport since v0.4.0 via vendored static mbedtls.

flows.title: SQL execution (L3 pipeline)
flows.summary: AdsExecuteSQL / AdsPrepareSQL drives the L3 pipeline: Lexer → Parser → Resolver → Planner → Executor. The Executor builds a transient Cursor backed by L4 engine reads.

steps.action: build AST (joins, subqueries, CTEs, window fns)
bind names → table/column handles via Catalog
choose join order, push WHERE down, plan ORDER/GROUP
materialise / stream rows; spill via L4 Cursor
result cursor (wire returns cursor table-id; in-proc returns Statement-bound cursor)
AdsFetch / AdsGetField over the SQL cursor

flows.notes: Full Advantage SQL dialect — INNER/LEFT/RIGHT/FULL OUTER, correlated subqueries, GROUP BY+HAVING, UNION, window fns, CTEs, CASE, AEP-hosted stored procedures.

flows.title: Studio web console (HTTP)
flows.summary: The same Studio SPA is served by two hosts: openads_serverd (Remote Server mode) and ace64.dll itself (LocalServer mode). Each REST request opens a short-lived ABI connection — Studio is just another ABI consumer.
flows.trigger: GET http://host:6263/  (or AdsStudioStart(8080, dir) in LocalServer mode)
flows.notes: Mode badge (rc10) distinguishes LocalServer (green 🏠) from Remote Server (blue 🌐) via /api/health's `mode` field. Default bind 127.0.0.1 to keep the console off the LAN unless opted in.

flows.title: Advantage Optimized Filter (Rushmore / AOF)
flows.summary: AdsSetAOF parses an xBase/SQL-flavoured boolean cond, evaluates it once, installs a per-record bitmap, and walks only the visible set on subsequent Skip / GoTop. Index-accelerated leaves push the range scan into CDX / NTX.

steps.action: AdsSkip / AdsGotoTop  (now O(M) over visible set)
AdsClearAOF → drop predicate + sequence in lockstep

flows.notes: On 100k-row TAG eq-walk, rc12 sparse-bitmap Skip is 13.4× faster than rc10 full-scan baseline.

flows.title: Memo / blob I/O (FPT / DBT / ADM)
flows.summary: Memo columns are out-of-line in a sibling FPT / DBT / ADM file. GetField/SetField on an M/B/P/Q cell routes through MemoStore to fetch (or extend) the block chain.

steps.action: block ptr from DBF row → MemoStore::read(block_id)
read N blocks from .fpt/.dbt/.adm (no 65 534-byte cap since rc4)
raw bytes; HTTP path may wrap as {_b64,_size,_truncated} if non-UTF-8
AdsSetMemo: extend chain, mark row dirty, write through (or hold for TxLog)

flows.notes: Validated bit-perfect at any size via openads_memo_stress (1.0 GB FPT, 12 000 rows × 2 fields, 0 mismatches).

flows.title: ARIES transaction (BeginTx / Commit / Rollback)
flows.summary: AdsBeginTransaction nests a frame on the Session's Tx stack. Mutations (AppendRecord, SetField, DeleteRecord, …) write redo/undo records to TxLog. Commit flushes; Rollback rewinds via undo chain.

steps.action: push TxFrame; pin Tables for tx scope
mutate page; append (lsn, before, after) to TxLog
TxLog::commit → fsync; release locks; pop frame
TxLog::rollback → replay undo chain in reverse

flows.notes: Crash recovery on next connect: TxLog::recover replays winners, undoes losers via the LSN tail.

flows.title: Wire-protocol session opcodes (TCP/TLS)
flows.summary: Concrete on-the-wire opcode sequence for a one-table read session. Every frame is `BE u32 length` + `u8 opcode` + payload.
flows.trigger: AdsConnect60("tcp://host:6262/data") followed by AdsOpenTable + walk + AdsCloseTable
flows.notes: TCP_NODELAY enabled since M12.20 (rc18) — wire is strict ping-pong, Nagle accumulation was pure latency tax.
